foods.name: Poha Jalebi
foods.description: A classic Madhya Pradesh morning: soft savoury poha followed by warm jalebi.

foods.name: Kachori
foods.description: Crisp, spiced and satisfying; best shared rather than eaten before a long darshan queue.

foods.name: Sabudana Khichdi
foods.description: Sago cooked with peanuts, potato and mild spices, widely enjoyed during fasting days.

foods.name: Dal Bafla
foods.description: A rich regional meal of baked wheat dumplings, dal, ghee, chutney and often churma.

foods.name: Bhutte Ka Kees
foods.description: Grated corn gently cooked with milk, mustard and spices; comforting and distinctly Malwa.

foods.name: Garadu
foods.description: Crisp fried yam tossed with tangy spices, especially popular during cooler months.

foods.name: Malpua
foods.description: Soft syrup-soaked pancakes often served warm as a festive or temple-town treat.

foods.name: Rabdi
foods.description: Slow-reduced sweet milk best eaten fresh from a clean, high-turnover shop.

foods.name: Shikanji
foods.description: Indore-style shikanji is a thick, creamy drink—not the lemon drink visitors may expect.

foods.name: Thandai
foods.description: A festive milk drink with nuts and spices. Families should clearly confirm ingredients before ordering.

indoreHighlights.title: Sarafa Bazaar
indoreHighlights.subtitle: Late-night food market
indoreHighlights.description: A jewellery market by day that transforms into a celebrated vegetarian food street at night.

indoreHighlights.title: Chappan Dukan
indoreHighlights.subtitle: Easy family food walk
indoreHighlights.description: A more structured row of food outlets suited to families wanting variety without a very late night.

indoreHighlights.title: Street Food Culture
indoreHighlights.subtitle: Taste small portions
indoreHighlights.description: Indore rewards curiosity, but the best strategy is to share small servings across several stalls.

indoreHighlights.title: Late-Night Food
indoreHighlights.subtitle: Plan it on a non-darshan night
indoreHighlights.description: Keep late-night eating separate from an early Mahakal or long road-travel morning.

safetyTips.title: Drink safe water
safetyTips.description: Use sealed or reliably filtered water and avoid uncertain ice during crowded periods.

safetyTips.title: Eat light before darshan
safetyTips.description: Avoid oily or heavy meals before long queues, early rituals or road transfers.

safetyTips.title: Carry ORS or electrolytes
safetyTips.description: Heat, walking and crowds can dehydrate visitors faster than expected.

safetyTips.title: Plan for elders and children
safetyTips.description: Carry familiar snacks, check spice levels and keep regular meal timing where possible.

safetyTips.title: Choose clean busy stalls
safetyTips.description: High turnover and freshly cooked hot food are usually safer choices than food sitting uncovered.

foodItinerary.title: Ujjain Breakfast
foodItinerary.plan: Poha Jalebi or Sabudana Khichdi after darshan, followed by safe drinking water.
foodItinerary.tip: Keep the portion light if another temple queue follows.

foodItinerary.title: Light Food Before Temple Visit
foodItinerary.plan: Choose fruit you can peel, simple khichdi, toast or a small familiar snack.
foodItinerary.tip: Avoid experimental, very spicy or heavy food before a long wait.

foodItinerary.title: Indore Evening Food Walk
foodItinerary.plan: Start at Chappan Dukan or choose a focused Sarafa shortlist and share small portions.
foodItinerary.tip: Schedule it on a night without an early darshan or long transfer next morning.
